candidates.title: VP-VLA: Visual Prompting as an Interface for Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models typically map visual observations and linguistic instructions directly to robotic control signals. candidates.title: RoboAlign: Learning Test-Time Reasoning for Language-Action Alignment in Vision-Language-Action Models
candidates.abstract_snippet: Improving embodied reasoning in multimodal-large-language models (MLLMs) is essential for building vision-language-action models (VLAs) on top of them to readily translate multimodal understanding into low-level actions. candidates.title: ProbeFlow: Training-Free Adaptive Flow Matching for Vision-Language-Action Models

candidates.title: Beyond Dense Futures: World Models as Structured Planners for Robotic Manipulation
candidates.abstract_snippet: Recent world-model-based Vision-Language-Action (VLA) architectures have improved robotic manipulation through predictive visual foresight. candidates.title: UniDex: A Robot Foundation Suite for Universal Dexterous Hand Control from Egocentric Human Videos

candidates.title: SmoothVLA: Aligning Vision-Language-Action Models with Physical Constraints via Intrinsic Smoothness Optimization
candidates.abstract_snippet: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a powerful paradigm for robotic manipulation. candidates.title: StageCraft: Execution Aware Mitigation of Distractor and Obstruction Failures in VLA Models
